question_text: A kettle is 80% efficient. It uses 2000 J of electrical energy to heat water. Calculate how much energy is wasted as heat energy lost to the surroundings (not heating the water).
explanation: If the kettle is 80% efficient, then 80% of the 2000 J goes into useful energy (heating water) and 20% is wasted. Wasted energy = 20% of 2000 J = 0.20 × 2000 = 400 J. This wasted energy escapes as heat through the kettle's sides and as steam.
